tech.title: First American TV commercial airs in Boston
tech.description: An experimental television station in Boston includes a paid advertisement during a CBS broadcast. The moment hints at the future of television as an advertising-driven medium.

tech.title: Apollo 17 launches, final crewed Moon mission
tech.description: NASA launches Apollo 17, the last human mission to the Moon. Astronauts conduct extensive scientific exploration during the Apollo program’s final chapter.

tech.title: NASA declares Mars Polar Lander lost
tech.description: NASA announces the Mars Polar Lander is likely destroyed after communication attempts fail. The loss leads to improvements in future Mars landing systems.

tech.title: The first iPod sells out nationwide
tech.description: Apple’s iPod sells out across the U.S. during the holiday season. The success marks the beginning of Apple’s dominance in portable digital music.

tech.title: SpaceX advances reusable rocket landing technology
tech.description: SpaceX completes key tests supporting vertical rocket landings. The work helps pave the way for reusable orbital rockets.

history.title: Delaware becomes the first U.S. state
history.description: Delaware ratifies the U.S. Constitution, becoming the first state to join the Union. The milestone earns it the nickname “The First State.”

history.title: Lewis and Clark begin winter at Fort Clatsop
history.description: The Corps of Discovery begins wintering at Fort Clatsop after reaching the Pacific Ocean. The fort serves as a base before the return journey east.

history.title: Attack on Pearl Harbor shocks the world
history.description: Japan launches a surprise attack on Pearl Harbor, killing over 2,400 Americans. The attack propels the United States into World War II.

history.title: Spitak earthquake devastates Armenia
history.description: A powerful earthquake strikes Armenia, killing tens of thousands. The disaster triggers a massive international relief effort.

crime_and_justice.title: First U.S. lethal injection execution carried out
crime_and_justice.description: Texas executes Charles Brooks Jr., marking the first lethal injection in U.S. history. The method later becomes the nation’s primary execution technique.

crime_and_justice.title: Long Island Rail Road massacre
crime_and_justice.description: A gunman opens fire on a commuter train, killing six people. The attack sparks national debate on gun violence and public safety.

crime_and_justice.title: Charlottesville attacker convicted of murder
crime_and_justice.description: James Alex Fields Jr. is convicted for killing a counterprotester during the Charlottesville rally. The verdict draws nationwide attention.

crime_and_justice.title: CIA domestic spying revelations surface
crime_and_justice.description: Reports reveal that the CIA conducted domestic surveillance on Americans. The disclosures lead to congressional investigations and reforms.

war_and_conflict.title: U.S. declares war on Austria-Hungary
war_and_conflict.description: The United States expands its role in World War I by declaring war on Austria-Hungary. The move strengthens Allied forces.

war_and_conflict.title: North Vietnam launches rocket attack on Saigon
war_and_conflict.description: Rockets strike Saigon, killing dozens during the Vietnam War. The attack underscores the conflict’s intensity in its final years.

war_and_conflict.title: NATO enforces Dayton Peace Agreement
war_and_conflict.description: NATO begins peacekeeping operations following the Dayton Agreement. The action effectively ends the Bosnian War.

births.title: Noam Chomsky born
births.description: Influential linguist and philosopher known for shaping modern language theory and political thought.

births.title: Larry Bird born
births.description: NBA legend and Boston Celtics star widely regarded as one of basketball’s greatest players.

deaths.title: Chuck Yeager dies
deaths.description: Legendary test pilot and first person to break the sound barrier passes away at age 97.
